explanation_box.text: Perhaps longer tails are more attractive to females?
Perhaps its bright colors attract more females.
Perhaps it is the toes.  Perhaps clawed toes and curved toes are a representation of fertility.
Clawed toes, high fertility like before.

prompt_continuous: Reptiles varied in whether they were GREEN or RED. Did being GREEN or RED make a difference to fertility? If you did not notice this feature or believe it did not make a difference to fertility, please hit no and proceed.
question_text: Did color matter to the fertility of reptiles?

prompt_continuous: Reptiles varied in whether they had ROUND feet or POINTY, CURLY feet. Did having CURLY/POINTY or ROUND feet make a difference to fertility? If you did not notice this feature or believe it did not make a difference to fertility, please hit no and proceed.
question_text: Did foot shape matter to the fertility of reptiles?

feedback_text.text: Until the very end when it asked if color affected fertility and it put those colors side by side, I never noticed there was a light and dark variant.  The light and dark variants were too similar for me to notice during the actual task, but perhaps that was the point, lol.  I had, based off my understanding of the instructions, thought the goal was merely to identify high and low fertility, not the exact amount of fertility.  So, I was surprised when I was asked to estimate their fertility by an exact amount rather than by high or low.
